message:
Reorganize installation documentation into dedicated section

- Create new Installation guide section with uvx as the primary method
- Add tabs for alternative installation methods: Docker, curl installer,
  Linux packages (Debian/RPM/Nix), and tarball
- Move account-creation guide into installation section
- Update cross-references throughout docs to point to new /guides/installation
  paths
- Add CLI tip in quickstart for command-line users
- Update sidebar to reflect new Installation section structure

This improves the user experience by consolidating all installation
instructions in one location and highlighting uvx as the recommended
approach for most users.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,147 @@
+---
+title: Overview
+---
+
+import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
+
+The Tenzir package includes two binaries:
+
+- **`tenzir`**: Execute a single pipeline from the command line
+- **`tenzir-node`**: Run a persistent node for continuous pipeline execution
+
+These binaries run pipelines on your machine. To manage pipelines through a web
+interface, [create an account](/guides/installation/create-account) and use the
+[platform](https://app.tenzir.com). See the [architecture
+overview](/explanations/architecture) to learn how they fit together.
+
+Choose the installation method that fits your use case.
+
+## uvx
+
+Run pipelines instantly without installation:
+
+```sh
+uvx tenzir 'from {x: 42}'
+```
+
+Works on macOS (Apple Silicon) and Linux (ARM/AMD).
+
+:::note[Running the latest version]
+The `@latest` suffix ensures you always get the most recent Tenzir release from
+PyPI. We recommend using `uvx tenzir@latest` to stay up to date. If startup time
+is a concern, omit `@latest` and manually refresh the cache with
+`uvx tenzir@latest` when you want updates.
+:::
+
+## curl installer
+
+Run the installer and follow the instructions to download the binaries, and
+optionally start a node:
+
+```sh
+curl https://get.tenzir.app | sh
+```
+
+The installer script asks for confirmation before performing the installation.
+It uses the package manager of your Linux distribution to install the Tenzir
+package. This typically also creates a [systemd](https://systemd.io) unit and
+starts the node automatically.
+
+## Linux
+
+<Tabs>
+<TabItem value="debian" label="Debian" icon="linux">
+
+Download the latest [Debian package][tenzir-debian-package] and install it via
+`dpkg`:
+
+```sh
+dpkg -i tenzir-static-amd64-linux.deb
+```
+
+You can uninstall the Tenzir package via `apt-get remove tenzir`. Use `purge`
+instead of `remove` if you also want to delete the state directory and leave no
+trace.
+
+[tenzir-debian-package]: https://github.com/tenzir/tenzir/releases/latest/download/tenzir-static-amd64-linux.deb
+
+</TabItem>
+<TabItem value="rpm" label="RPM-based" icon="linux">
+
+Download the latest [RPM package][tenzir-rpm-package] and install it via `rpm`:
+
+```sh
+rpm -i tenzir-static-amd64-linux.rpm
+```
+
+[tenzir-rpm-package]: https://github.com/tenzir/tenzir/releases/latest/download/tenzir-static-amd64-linux.rpm
+
+</TabItem>
+<TabItem value="nix" label="Nix" icon="nix">
+
+Run Tenzir ad-hoc without installing:
+
+```sh
+nix run github:tenzir/tenzir/latest
+```
+
+Or enter a shell with Tenzir available:
+
+```sh
+nix shell github:tenzir/tenzir/latest -c tenzir 'from {x: 42}'
+```
+
+Install a Tenzir Node by adding `github:tenzir/tenzir/latest` to your flake
+inputs, or use your preferred method to include third-party modules on classic
+NixOS.
+
+:::note[Pre-built only!]
+The default attributes of the flake contain some closed source plugins and will
+fail to download locally. The flake is only for public consumption together with
+the [Tenzir cachix cache](https://tenzir.cachix.org).
+:::
+
+</TabItem>
+<TabItem value="docker" label="Docker" icon="seti:docker">
+
+Run pipelines in a container without installing anything locally:
+
+```sh
+docker run --rm -it tenzir/tenzir 'from {x: 42}'
+```
+
+</TabItem>
+<TabItem value="tarball" label="Tarball" icon="seti:folder">
+
+Download a tarball with our [static binary][tenzir-tarball] for all Linux
+distributions and unpack it into `/opt/tenzir`:
+
+```sh
+tar xzf tenzir-static-x86_64-linux.tar.gz -C /
+```
+
+[tenzir-tarball]: https://github.com/tenzir/tenzir/releases/latest/download/tenzir-static-x86_64-linux.tar.gz
+
+We also offer prebuilt statically linked binaries for every Git commit to the
+`main` branch:
+
+```sh
+curl -O https://storage.googleapis.com/tenzir-dist-public/packages/main/tarball/tenzir-static-main.gz
+```
+
+</TabItem>
+</Tabs>
+
+## Build from source
+
+You can also [build from source](/guides/development/build-from-source). Note
+that this excludes proprietary plugins that ship with our prebuilt packages.
+
+## Next steps
+
+- [Run pipelines](/guides/basic-usage/run-pipelines): Execute TQL pipelines from
+  the CLI
+- [Deploy a node](/guides/node-setup/deploy-a-node): Run a persistent node for
+  continuous operation
+- [Configuration](/explanations/configuration): Learn how to configure Tenzir
+  with files and environment variables
